parameters.content:
## Automated Article Scheduling

**1. Fast Bulk Article Generation**
Quickly create multiple AI-generated articles.
Efficiently streamline content creation.
Reduces manual effort while maintaining quality.

**2. Workflow Testing Before Execution**
Manually test the workflow for debugging.
Ensure each step runs as expected.
Optimize before full automation.

**3. Automated & Randomized Publishing**
Schedule posts at predefined intervals.
Introduce random delays for a natural posting pattern.
Prevents overly predictable publishing behavior.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## AI Content Generating

**Automatic Content & Keyword Generation

- Use your own prompt to start
- ChatGPT generates full-length articles with structured headings.
- Extracts relevant image search keywords for visual enhancement.
- To implement this, add the following prompt (green note) below your workflow:

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
(YOUR PROMPT) **Image Search Keywords (For Visual Alignment)** - Automatically generates 3-5 English keywords for image searches based on the article content. - Keywords should be specific objects, locations, or atmospheres rather than abstract concepts. **Article Formatting Requirements** 1️⃣ Title (H1): Ensure unique and trend-driven headlines. 2️⃣ H2 / H3 Subheadings: Structure content in an SEO-optimized format. 3️⃣ Article Structure (Enhanced Readability) ** Introduction ** - Go straight to the point, avoiding excessive background. - Use question hooks or market trend data to engage readers. ** Core Content ** - Include at least three knowledge points to ensure depth. - Balance short and long sentences for better flow. ** Conclusion ** - Avoid generic AI-style summaries; instead, provide insights or actionable takeaways. - Optionally include a CTA (Call to Action). ** HTML Formatting ** Ensure the article is properly structured in HTML format: - Headings: Use

,

,

appropriately. - Paragraphs: Enclose text within

. - Emphasized Words: Use to highlight key terms. - Lists: Use

    and
  • for bullet points. Ensure a clean, well-structured output instead of plain text. ### **Final JSON Format Ensure the output follows this structure: { "title": "{Generate an H1 title that aligns with market trends, ensures high click-through rates, and follows keyword strategy}", "content": "{Generate a complete HTML article including H1, H2, H3 headings, paragraphs, lists, etc.}", "keywords": ["{Image search keyword 1}", "{Image search keyword 2}", "{Image search keyword 3}", "{Image search keyword 4}", "{Image search keyword 5}"] }

parameters.content:
## Introduction: WordPress automatically publishes posts and inserts the first image

It is **highly recommended to install the Featured Image from URL (FIFU) plugin** and enable:

**Auto > Set Featured Media Automatically from Content.** before you generate contents.

parameters.content:
## Automated Article Scheduling

**1. Fast Bulk Article Generation**
Quickly create multiple AI-generated articles.
Efficiently streamline content creation.
Reduces manual effort while maintaining quality.

**2. Workflow Testing Before Execution**
Manually test the workflow for debugging.
Ensure each step runs as expected.
Optimize before full automation.

**3. Automated & Randomized Publishing**
Schedule posts at predefined intervals.
Introduce random delays for a natural posting pattern.
Prevents overly predictable publishing behavior.

## AI Content Generating

**Automatic Content & Keyword Generation

- Use your own prompt to start
- ChatGPT generates full-length articles with structured headings.
- Extracts relevant image search keywords for visual enhancement.
- To implement this, add the following prompt (green note) below your workflow:

(YOUR PROMPT) **Image Search Keywords (For Visual Alignment)** - Automatically generates 3-5 English keywords for image searches based on the article content. - Keywords should be specific objects, locations, or atmospheres rather than abstract concepts. **Article Formatting Requirements** 1️⃣ Title (H1): Ensure unique and trend-driven headlines. 2️⃣ H2 / H3 Subheadings: Structure content in an SEO-optimized format. 3️⃣ Article Structure (Enhanced Readability) ** Introduction ** - Go straight to the point, avoiding excessive background. - Use question hooks or market trend data to engage readers. ** Core Content ** - Include at least three knowledge points to ensure depth. - Balance short and long sentences for better flow. ** Conclusion ** - Avoid generic AI-style summaries; instead, provide insights or actionable takeaways. - Optionally include a CTA (Call to Action). ** HTML Formatting ** Ensure the article is properly structured in HTML format: - Headings: Use

,

,

appropriately. - Paragraphs: Enclose text within

. - Emphasized Words: Use to highlight key terms. - Lists: Use

    and
  • for bullet points. Ensure a clean, well-structured output instead of plain text. ### **Final JSON Format Ensure the output follows this structure: { "title": "{Generate an H1 title that aligns with market trends, ensures high click-through rates, and follows keyword strategy}", "content": "{Generate a complete HTML article including H1, H2, H3 headings, paragraphs, lists, etc.}", "keywords": ["{Image search keyword 1}", "{Image search keyword 2}", "{Image search keyword 3}", "{Image search keyword 4}", "{Image search keyword 5}"] }

## Introduction: WordPress automatically publishes posts and inserts the first image

It is **highly recommended to install the Featured Image from URL (FIFU) plugin** and enable:

**Auto > Set Featured Media Automatically from Content.** before you generate contents.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "1. Auto Start".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Verify the settings of the trigger node "2. When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the scheduler node "3. Schedule Your Posts".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Create posts on Wordpress" node (n8n-nodes-base.wordpress) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Random Wait" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Save to Sheet" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Automated Image Retrieval from Pexels" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate AI Content" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
